content.text: Build a design system for my personal blog. Support light and dark mode.

I’ll create this directly in your Figma file as a new design-system page with color tokens, typography, core components, and both light/dark modes. First I’m connecting to the plugin and checking the current document state.

text.text: Joined channel "vibma" on port 3055. Call `ping` now to verify the Figma plugin is connected.

content.text: Connection is good. I’m now creating a dedicated `Design System` page and then I’ll lay out tokens and components in structured sections.

I’ve set up a tokenized color system with Light/Dark modes using variables. Next I’m building out foundations and reusable UI components on the page, then I’ll pin one preview frame to each mode so you can see both themes side-by-side.

items.text: Body / 18 Regular - Thoughtful writing on design, technology, and creative work.
Body / 16 Regular - Perfect for long-form blog content and readability.
Small / 14 Medium - Metadata, tags, and helper text.
Caption / 12 Medium - Fine print and timestamps.

content.text:
Built directly in your Figma file on a new page: `Design System`.

What’s included:

- Light/dark theming via a `Theme` variable collection with `Light` + `Dark` modes.
- Color tokens:
  - `color/background`, `color/surface`, `color/text/primary`, `color/text/secondary`
  - `color/brand`, `color/brand/contrast`, `color/border`, `color/surface-muted`
  - `color/success`, `color/warning`
- Typography styles:
  - `Type/Display`, `Type/H1`, `Type/H2`
  - `Type/Body/L`, `Type/Body/M`, `Type/Body/S`, `Type/Caption`, `Type/Button`
- Foundations section with token swatches + typography scale examples.
- Components section with reusable components:
  - `Button` variant set (`Primary`, `Secondary`)
  - `Card/Post Preview`
  - `Nav/Desktop`
  - `Input/Email`
- Theme previews section:
  - `Light Mode Preview` frame pinned to Light mode
  - `Dark Mode Preview` frame pinned to Dark mode
  - Same components shown in both modes using shared tokens

If you want, I can next add a second component pass for blog-specific patterns (article hero, post list item, tag chip, pagination, footer, and mobile nav) using the same token system.

result:
I’ll create this directly in your Figma file as a new design-system page with color tokens, typography, core components, and both light/dark modes. First I’m connecting to the plugin and checking the current document state.Connection is good. I’m now creating a dedicated `Design System` page and then I’ll lay out tokens and components in structured sections.I’ve set up a tokenized color system with Light/Dark modes using variables. Next I’m building out foundations and reusable UI components on the page, then I’ll pin one preview frame to each mode so you can see both themes side-by-side.I’m now building reusable blog components (buttons, card, nav bar, and input) and then converting them into components so you can instantiate them anywhere.Built directly in your Figma file on a new page: `Design System`.

What’s included:

- Light/dark theming via a `Theme` variable collection with `Light` + `Dark` modes.
- Color tokens:
  - `color/background`, `color/surface`, `color/text/primary`, `color/text/secondary`
  - `color/brand`, `color/brand/contrast`, `color/border`, `color/surface-muted`
  - `color/success`, `color/warning`
- Typography styles:
  - `Type/Display`, `Type/H1`, `Type/H2`
  - `Type/Body/L`, `Type/Body/M`, `Type/Body/S`, `Type/Caption`, `Type/Button`
- Foundations section with token swatches + typography scale examples.
- Components section with reusable components:
  - `Button` variant set (`Primary`, `Secondary`)
  - `Card/Post Preview`
  - `Nav/Desktop`
  - `Input/Email`
- Theme previews section:
  - `Light Mode Preview` frame pinned to Light mode
  - `Dark Mode Preview` frame pinned to Dark mode
  - Same components shown in both modes using shared tokens

If you want, I can next add a second component pass for blog-specific patterns (article hero, post list item, tag chip, pagination, footer, and mobile nav) using the same token system.
